mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over 3 months signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly payroll growth exceeding 150k signals robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in periods with PAYEMS > 150k fall below 0.5% in 60% of cases, or the signal's IC drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent decline in housing starts (HOUST) and rising consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals stagflationary pressures that depress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in stagflation scenarios exceed -1.5% in 70% of cases, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 102.5 signals strong manufacturing activity that drives corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in INDPRO expansion phases fall below 0.8% in 50% of cases, or the signal's IC drops below 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the federal funds rate above 4.5% triggers a risk-off sentiment that reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in rate hike environments exceed -0.3% in 60% of cases, or the signal's IC becomes negative.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising residential construction (RSAFS) and declining GDP growth (GDPC1) signals a housing market bubble burst that depresses equity prices, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in bubble burst scenarios exceed -2.0% in 70% of cases, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes insignificant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent drop in unemployment (UNRATE) below 4.0% and rising wage growth (PAYEMS) signals tight labor market dynamics that push inflation higher, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in wage-push scenarios exceed -1.0% in 60% of cases, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes negative.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 102.0 signals weakening manufacturing activity that reduces corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in production contraction phases exceed -1.5% in 50% of cases, or the signal's IC drops below 0.03.
